hero.thesis: Launch an online music lessons service, leveraging your skills in music and tutoring to earn $60–$100/hr. Secure 10 students for a full pipeline and target $2,800 MRR with minimal startup costs.
hero.marketSize: The global online music education market is projected to reach $10 billion by 2025.
hero.payingEvidence: Thousands of students on platforms like LessonHype and MusicianConnect already pay $50–$150/hr for music lessons.

ninetyDay.title: Foundation Setup

actions.action: Register your business name and set up a simple website using Webflow.
actions.success: Business is registered and Webflow site is live.

actions.action: Create a pricing plan and set up payment processing via Stripe.
actions.success: Stripe account is active and linked to your business bank account.

actions.action: Join relevant music tutoring forums and communities like Reddit's r/musiclessons and MusicianConnect Discord.
actions.success: Joined two communities and introduced yourself.

ninetyDay.metric: Website live with payment processing and community presence.

ninetyDay.title: First Outreach

actions.action: Craft your first outreach email using the template provided.
Send personalized outreach to 30 potential students from LinkedIn and forums.
Follow up with anyone who hasn't replied to your initial email.

ninetyDay.metric: Receive at least 3 trial lesson bookings.

ninetyDay.title: Refinement and Expansion

actions.action: Collect feedback from trial lessons and refine your teaching approach.
Optimize your website and add testimonials from trial students.
Expand reach by posting content in music education groups on Facebook and LinkedIn.

ninetyDay.metric: Convert 3 trial lessons into paying students.

ninetyDay.title: Community Engagement

actions.action: Host a free introductory music workshop in a Discord server.
Engage daily in online music communities, offering tips and free advice.
Invite workshop attendees to book a trial lesson.

ninetyDay.metric: Gain 2 new paying students from workshop attendees.

actions.action: Start a weekly music tips newsletter using ConvertKit.
actions.success: Newsletter set up and first issue sent.

actions.action: Create and publish a blog post about learning music online.
Share the blog post in relevant online communities.
Recruit a part-time assistant to help with scheduling and admin tasks.
Develop a referral program offering discounts to students who bring new clients.
Promote the referral program in all communication channels.
actions.success: Promotion sent to all current students.

ninetyDay.title: Advanced Marketing

actions.action: Run a small Facebook ad campaign targeting local music enthusiasts.
Analyze ad performance and adjust targeting as needed.
Host a Q&A session on a music education YouTube channel (audio only).
actions.success: Q&A session completed with 20 live listeners.

ninetyDay.metric: Acquire 2 students from ads and Q&A.

actions.action: Redesign your website for better user experience using Framer.
Collect video testimonials from 3 satisfied students (audio only).
Launch a social proof campaign on social media showcasing testimonials.
Implement a loyalty program offering discounts for long-term students.
Send a feedback form to all current students.
Analyze feedback and make necessary adjustments to your service.

ninetyDay.title: Expanding Reach

actions.action: List your services on MusicianConnect and LessonHub.
Reach out to local schools and offer group lesson packages.
Network with local music stores for potential partnerships.

ninetyDay.metric: Secure 1 partnership with a local school or store.

actions.action: Review your financials and optimize expenses using Notion.
Adjust pricing strategy based on market demand.
Prepare a quarterly report for future planning.

ninetyDay.title: Future Planning

actions.action: Set goals for the next quarter and outline a plan to achieve them.
Identify potential new services or lessons to offer.
Schedule a review meeting with your assistant to discuss improvements.

ninetyDay.metric: Create a roadmap for the next 3 months.

toolStack.name: Webflow
toolStack.why: For creating a professional and easy-to-update website.

toolStack.name: ConvertKit
toolStack.why: To manage and grow your email newsletter list.

toolStack.name: Stripe
toolStack.why: For seamless payment processing from students.

toolStack.name: Framer
toolStack.why: To enhance your website's design and user experience.
toolStack.pricing: Free for personal use, $15/month for pro features.

toolStack.name: Notion
toolStack.why: To organize business operations and finances.
toolStack.pricing: Free for personal use, $8/month for pro features.

firstTenCustomers.script:
Hi [Name],

I'm [Your Name], a passionate music tutor with years of experience helping students excel in [Niche]. I'm currently offering personalized online music lessons and I'm reaching out to see if you or someone you know might be interested.

For a limited time, I'm offering a free trial lesson to new students. If you're interested, let's schedule a call!

Best,
[Your Name]
firstTenCustomers.cadence: Initial email on Monday, follow-up on Thursday, second follow-up the following Monday.

pricing.growth: $80/hour with homework assignments and lesson plans.
pricing.premium: $100/hour with personalized lesson plans and monthly progress reports.
pricing.raisingRules: Raise prices 10% every 6 months or when demand exceeds supply by 20%.

failureModes: Ignoring feedback: Leads to student dissatisfaction.
Neglecting online presence: Limits visibility and growth.
Over-promising: Leads to burnout and poor service.
Failure to niche: Blends into a crowded market.
Ignoring financials: Leads to cash flow issues.
Poor marketing: Results in lack of students.

setup.llc: Use Stripe Atlas to form an LLC. Cost: $500.
setup.payments: Set up Stripe for seamless online payments. Link to your business bank account.
setup.contracts: Use Lovable to create simple, reusable service agreements with students.
setup.insurance: Consider TutorGuard for liability insurance. Cost: $20/month.

scaling: Once you've hit your target MRR and feel the strain of juggling too many tasks, it's time to scale. Start by hiring a part-time assistant to handle scheduling, admin, and customer service. Look for someone with experience in the music or education field who can also help with content creation. Gradually increase your team by hiring additional tutors to expand your offerings and handle more students. Use tools like Notion to keep your team organized and focused on goals. As your team grows, consider investing in a more robust CRM system to manage student relationships and ensure quality service remains high as you scale.
